question_text: A student makes an electromagnet by wrapping wire around an iron nail and connecting it to a battery. Which change would make the electromagnet stronger?

options.label: B
options.text: Wrapping more turns of wire around the nail

options.label: C
options.text: Using a plastic rod instead of an iron nail

explanation: More turns of wire create a stronger magnetic field because the magnetic effect of each loop adds together. Iron is also a good core material, and the electromagnet needs current flowing through it to work.
